contradictions.claim_a: Exceptions to any requirement in this policy must be submitted through the Security Exception Request Form to the Information Security team, with a documented business justification and proposed compensating controls.
contradictions.claim_b: Exception requests are submitted to the Cloud Governance Board through the Infrastructure Exception form, with a named business owner and compensating control.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Exceptions to any requirement in this policy must be submitted through the Security Exception Request Form to the Information Security team, with a documented business justification and proposed compensating controls.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Exception requests are submitted to the Cloud Governance Board through the Infrastructure Exception form, with a named business owner and compensating control.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires exceptions to be submitted to the Information Security team, while Claim B requires exceptions to be submitted to the Cloud Governance Board.

contradictions.claim_a: Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher.
contradictions.claim_b: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated and blocked at the gateway
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated and blocked at the gateway
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires the use of TLS 1.2 or higher, while Claim B blocks TLS 1.0 and 1.1, implying the use of TLS 1.2 or higher is also required.

contradictions.claim_a: Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher.
contradictions.claim_b: Data in transit must use TLS 1.2 or higher
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Data in transit must use TLS 1.2 or higher
contradictions.rationale: Claim A specifies that data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher, while Claim B is more general and does not specify the scope of services.

contradictions.claim_a: This policy applies to all full-time and part-time employees, interns, and fixed-term contract staff engaged directly by Meridian Dynamics Corporation.
contradictions.claim_b: This policy applies to all full-time employees, contingent workers, contractors, and third-party service providers who access Meridian Dynamics systems, networks, or data, regardless of location or device ownership.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: This policy applies to all full-time and part-time employees, interns, and fixed-term contract staff engaged directly by Meridian Dynamics Corporation.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: This policy applies to all full-time employees, contingent workers, contractors, and third-party service providers who access Meridian Dynamics systems, networks, or data, regardless of location or device ownership.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A excludes contingent workers and contractors, while Claim B includes them. This discrepancy may lead to inconsistent application of the policy.

contradictions.claim_a: approval and are reviewed every 6 months.
contradictions.claim_b: Standard developer access follows a 6-month review cycle aligned to project assignment changes.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: approval and are reviewed every 6 months.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Standard developer access follows a 6-month review cycle aligned to project assignment changes.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A specifies a fixed 6-month review cycle for approval, whereas Claim B aligns the 6-month review cycle to project assignment changes, potentially leading to inconsistent review timing.

contradictions.claim_a: approval and are reviewed every 6 months.
contradictions.claim_b: Exceptions require CISO or delegate approval, are valid for a maximum of 12 months, and must be reassessed every 6 months for continued necessity.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: approval and are reviewed every 6 months.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: are valid for a maximum of 12 months
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires approval to be reviewed every 6 months, while Claim B allows exceptions to be valid for up to 12 months before reassessment.

contradictions.claim_a: Non-completion results in automatic account suspension pending remediation.
contradictions.claim_b: Repeated non-remediation is escalated to the responsible Director and may result in resource access suspension.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Non-completion results in automatic account suspension pending remediation.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Repeated non-remediation is escalated to the responsible Director and may result in resource access suspension.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A states that non-completion results in automatic account suspension, while Claim B states that repeated non-remediation may result in resource access suspension, which could be interpreted as a different consequence for non-completion.

contradictions.claim_a: Shared or generic credentials are prohibited except for break-glass emergency accounts, which require CISO pre-approval and are rotated immediately after use.
contradictions.claim_b: Changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines require CISO co-sign-off before publication.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Shared or generic credentials are prohibited except for break-glass emergency accounts, which require CISO pre-approval and are rotated immediately after use.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines require CISO co-sign-off before publication.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires immediate rotation of break-glass emergency accounts, while Claim B implies that changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines, which may involve break-glass emergency accounts, require CISO co-sign-off before publication, potentially delaying rotation.

contradictions.claim_a: It ensures consistent, legally defensible treatment of employees across all business units and geographies where the company operates.
contradictions.claim_b: It exists to reduce exposure to unauthorized access, data loss, and service disruption across all business units.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: It ensures consistent, legally defensible treatment of employees across all business units and geographies where the company operates.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: It exists to reduce exposure to unauthorized access, data loss, and service disruption across all business units.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A prioritizes consistent treatment of employees, while Claim B focuses on reducing exposure to security threats, indicating a potential conflict between employee treatment and security objectives.

contradictions.claim_a: Material changes to control requirements require joint sign-off from the CISO and General Counsel.
contradictions.claim_b: Changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines require CISO co-sign-off before publication.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Material changes to control requirements require joint sign-off from the CISO and General Counsel.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines require CISO co-sign-off before publication.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires joint sign-off from CISO and General Counsel for material changes, while Claim B only requires CISO co-sign-off for changes to approved provider lists or encryption baselines.

contradictions.claim_a: Exceptions require CISO or delegate approval, are valid for a maximum of 12 months, and must be reassessed every 6 months for continued necessity.
contradictions.claim_b: Exceptions are valid for a maximum of 6 months.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Exceptions require CISO or delegate approval, are valid for a maximum of 12 months, and must be reassessed every 6 months for continued necessity.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Exceptions are valid for a maximum of 6 months.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A states exceptions are valid for 12 months, while Claim B limits them to 6 months, indicating a conflict in exception duration.

contradictions.claim_a: All storage volumes, object storage buckets, and managed databases must enable encryption at rest using AES-256 with customer-managed keys for any resource holding Conﬁdential or Restricted data.
contradictions.claim_b: Data at rest classiﬁed as Conﬁdential or higher must use AES-256 encryption
contradictions.evidence_span_a: All storage volumes, object storage buckets, and managed databases must enable encryption at rest using AES-256 with customer-managed keys for any resource holding Conﬁdential or Restricted data.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Data at rest classiﬁed as Conﬁdential or higher must use AES-256 encryption
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires customer-managed keys for encryption at rest, while Claim B does not specify key management.
